zh.Streatlam and Stainton, Durham, England: 英國達勒姆斯特亞特拉姆 安德 斯泰頓
zh.Battle of Poitiers: 法國波特-哈倫斯維安波爾斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de la Bowes I於1330年出生在英國達勒姆斯特亞特拉姆 安德 斯泰頓時，他的父親Robert Bowes20歲，母親Elizabeth De Leybourne20歲。他和Maud de Dalton至少有2個兒子。他去世於1421年在英國達勒姆包維斯享年91歲。

zh-hans.Streatlam and Stainton, Durham, England: 英国達勒姆斯特亞特拉姆 安德 斯泰頓
zh-hans.Battle of Poitiers: 法国波特-哈倫斯維安波爾斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William de la Bowes I于1330年出生在英国達勒姆斯特亞特拉姆 安德 斯泰頓时，他的父亲Robert Bowes20岁，母亲Elizabeth De Leybourne20岁。他和Maud de Dalton至少有2个儿子。他去世于1421年在英国達勒姆包維斯享年91岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Bowes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Bowes (formerly in North Yorkshire, now in County Durham), or from some other place so called, the placename being derived from the plural of Old English boga ‘bow’, here referring to bends in a river. Alternatively, a topographic name for someone who lived ‘(at the) arches or bridge’. See Bow 1.
English: variant of Bow , with excrescent -s.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Buadhaigh (see Bogue ).

variants.name: Bow
meanings: Scottish and English: habitational name from any of various minor places called with Old English boga ‘bow, arch, vault’, i.e. an arched bridge. There are places so called in Midlothian, Middlesex, and Devon.
Irish and English: variant of   Bowes  .

Chinese: possibly from Cantonese form of the Chinese names 寶 (meaning ‘treasure’) or 保 (meaning ‘protect’), which were monosyllabic personal names or part of disyllabic personal names of some early Chinese immigrants in the US.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de la Bowes I was born in 1330, in Streatlam and Stainton, Durham, England, his father, Robert Bowes, was 20 and his mother, Elizabeth De Leybourne, was 20. He had at least 2 sons with Maud de Dalton. He died in 1421, in Bowes, Durham, England, at the age of 91.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de la Bowes I est né en 1330, à Streatlam and Stainton, Durham, Angleterre, son père, Robert Bowes, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth De Leybourne, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Maud de Dalton. Il est décédé en 1421, à Bowes, Durham, Angleterre, à l’âge de 91 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de la Bowes I 1330 in Streatlam and Stainton, Durham, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Bowes 20 und seine Mutter Elizabeth De Leybourne 20. Er hatte mit Maud de Dalton mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1421 im Alter von 91 Jahren in Bowes, Durham, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de la Bowes I è nato il nel 1330 nel Streatlam and Stainton, Durham, England, il suo padre, Robert Bowes, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth De Leybourne, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Maud de Dalton. Morì nel 1421, nel Bowes, Durham, England, all'età di 91 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William de la Bowes Iが1330年にイギリスダルハムストレアトラム アンド スティイントンで生まれた時，父Robert Bowesは20歳，母Elizabeth De Leybourneは20歳であった。彼はMaud de Daltonとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1421年にイギリスダルハムボウスで91歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de la Bowes I(이)가 1330년 영국 뒤람 스트리틀램 앤드 스테인턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Bowes(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth De Leybourne(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Maud de Dalton(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1421년 영국 뒤람 보우스에서 91세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de la Bowes I nasceu em 1330, em Streatlam and Stainton, Durham, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Bowes, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth De Leybourne, tinha 20 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Maud de Dalton. Ele faleceu em 1421, em Bowes, Durham, Inglaterra, com 91 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de la Bowes I родился в 1330 г. в местности стритлэм анд стеинтон, дурам, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Bowes было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth De Leybourne, 20. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Maud de Dalton. Он умер в 1421 г. в местности боуесь, дурам, Англия в возрасте 91 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William de la Bowes I nació en 1330, en Streatlam and Stainton, Durham, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Bowes, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Elizabeth De Leybourne, tenía 20 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Maud de Dalton. Murió en 1421, en Bowes, Durham, Inglaterra, a la edad de 91 años.
